114248.explanation: Coalition parties propose their own candidates for aldermen. For the CDA, quality matters more than gender.

114249.explanation: We support participation, and for that reason the CDA holds many conversations with residents and businesses.

114252.explanation: To maintain a liveable and enjoyable rural area, other developments must also be possible. This also helps prevent the deterioration of the countryside. Provided it fits within the zoning plan.

114253.explanation: Although we welcome such initiatives, investments in sustainable energy must basically pay for themselves. We do see a role for the municipality in terms of regulations or otherwise supporting such initiatives.

114255.explanation: We support the principle of the Distribution Act, where each municipality contributes a small share. When reception is necessary, we favor small-scale shelters at suitable locations.

114256.explanation: To arrange things as well as possible for both companies and seasonal workers themselves, housing near the workplace is most appropriate. This also minimizes the impact on the surroundings and allows the municipality and entrepreneurs to maintain control.

114264.explanation: To facilitate housing construction, all possibilities must be considered. If the land is in a good location and fits the environment, cooperation should be given for building homes on agricultural land.

114267.explanation: Due to the large amount of housing construction, facilities must also be maintained, including school buildings. Significant investments will need to continue in the coming years. Every area must retain a primary school. Education is crucial for the development of our children.

114268.explanation: The municipality subsidizes cultural associations, including music clubs. As CDA, we wholeheartedly support this.

114272.explanation: For the CDA, a safer living environment is paramount. Currently, there are sufficient municipal enforcement officers. However, clearer prioritization of tasks is needed to enforce on the right matters.

114273.explanation: Camera surveillance can be a solution for nuisance in some places, such as schoolyards. However, it has limitations and adverse effects like privacy infringement. Thus, it is certainly not a solution for all problems and often just shifts the issue elsewhere.

114274.explanation: Unfortunately, these specific shelter locations are necessary. There are women’s shelters / safe houses, and we want to keep making these available.

114275.explanation: The municipality unfortunately cannot provide loans to all first-time buyers. This is because the municipality is not a bank and must maintain a healthy and stable budget. The CDA does promote starter loans from banks. Additionally, we focus on building many affordable homes.

114276.explanation: The housing cooperative already focuses on this as much as possible. This applies to 70% of the homes allocated. The CDA also supports this.

114278.explanation: Permanent residence is only allowed if the home has a 'residential destination'. The CDA wants to keep it that way; recreation is for recreation. Besides, occupancy of holiday homes is governed by national regulation.

114280.explanation: To a limited extent, people on social assistance can do socially valuable work and contribute to society this way. Especially now when it is increasingly difficult to find volunteers. This also helps people return to work faster.

114282.explanation: Fortunately, this is already arranged in Gemert-Bakel. Thanks to the CDA, the income limit for poverty assistance in our municipality is set at 120% of the national standard. This increase helps people get out of poverty faster. Children in poverty are also additionally helped by the Leergeld Foundation.

114284.explanation: There are increasing numbers of electric cars. Charging spots can be designated upon request. The CDA wants to run a pilot for shared use between regular parking spots and charging points. Preferably, we add spots rather than exchange them. This way, we maintain enough parking spaces.

114285.explanation: This decision has already been made by the municipal council. For the CDA, safety is the leading principle. Tailored solutions per road will also be needed to resolve bottlenecks and other issues.

114286.explanation: The municipality provides care that is suitable for everyone. If people prefer a different care provider based on their faith, that is of course possible. However, that may not be arranged or funded through the municipality.

114289.explanation: This tax, for example, benefits maintenance of run-off areas and emptying dog waste bins. If this tax is abolished, these costs will be borne by all residents, including those who do not own a dog.

114290.explanation: A lot of tourism is good for the municipality and local economy. Facilities are maintained through the tourist tax. At present, the level of the tourist tax is adequate.

114296.explanation: The Eendracht is the cultural center. After the secondary school, Macropedius College, is built, other major projects can be considered. The Eendracht is one such possible project. Of course, The Eendracht will remain suitable for cultural performances and activities.

114300.explanation: The CDA is against high residential towers. We do support somewhat taller buildings where appropriate around centers. We think of 5 to 6 stories. This is necessary to address the housing shortage and create affordable apartments.

114304.explanation: In principle, entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for preventing odor nuisance. Buyouts on a voluntary basis are possible if multiple objectives are involved, such as spatial development for housing or business parks.

114249.explanation: Residents must have input, but the frameworks within which that happens should be clear so expectations are managed.

114252.explanation: In general, as long as it does not pose obstacles for other companies.

114253.explanation: The municipality should act as a facilitator here but should not be ready with funds immediately.

114256.explanation: Seasonal workers must know to whom they are accountable, and residents must always have someone to address; therefore, housing on their own premises is best.

114264.explanation: As long as it does not pose obstacles for existing companies. Farms must always be allowed to be subdivided without a financial contribution, regardless of whether it is a listed building or not.

114267.explanation: The school buildings in Gemert-Bakel are in good condition precisely because the municipality values education highly.

114268.explanation: Even where parents truly cannot afford it, consideration can be given to a contribution.

114272.explanation: At the moment, Gemert-Bakel has 4 enforcement officers (BOAs), which should be increased to 6.

114273.explanation: If this adds value, but at the moment we do not see that added value.

114274.explanation: Currently there is no real demand for this and it could be better organized within the region as Peel municipalities.

114275.explanation: Financial support only drives prices up. It is better to ensure sufficient building locations and housing initiatives such as home splitting.

114276.explanation: Own residents first and only then others. The housing association should withdraw from Woonoesie.

114278.explanation: The Kanthoeve in Bakel must become habitable for house seekers but not for seasonal workers.

114280.explanation: This can be a nice stepping stone towards a work situation or a catalyst for a turnaround to a completely different function.

114282.explanation: People must first be identified and then it can be determined what help is needed, material or immaterial.

114284.explanation: If it turns out there is indeed demand, but initially this is the responsibility of the company and car owner.

114285.explanation: Where possible, but for smooth traffic flow this is not always desired or necessary.

114286.explanation: The assistance must be leading here, and if physical contact is problematic, it can be considered whether this is possible.

114289.explanation: The contribution may be lowered, but costs are also incurred, so abolishing it is a step too far.

114290.explanation: Keep it at the current level. We do not want to drive away tourists. However, good monitoring of this is necessary.

114296.explanation: Investments are being made in De Eendracht, and this is a facility that must remain in Gemert. Either De Eendracht or in a new Culture House.

114300.explanation: Initially no, but it should be examined on which residential locations apartments can be built and how tall.

114304.explanation: This depends on their location and the vision for the surroundings.

114248.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With equal numbers of men and women, therefore. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

114249.explanation: Our system only works if everyone can and wants to participate. As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a major role, a referendum can be a good and fair way to decide. We also support elected village councils and the youth municipal council.

114252.explanation: Farmers who want to expand their activities can apply for a permit. The municipality must then assess this with attention to the effects on the environment.

114253.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

114255.explanation: We adhere to the national mandate and our moral duty to provide a (temporary) home to some refugees. We do not exclude people for who they are, whom they love, what they believe, or where their parents' or grandparents' cradle stood.

114256.explanation: Those who bring people to our country must ensure decent accommodation. Research shows that the immediate vicinity of agricultural and horticultural businesses is often not a healthy place to live. Therefore, strict requirements must be set for housing on industrial sites. And we must enforce them.

114264.explanation: Farmers can already apply for a permit to build a house. The municipality must assess that permit with attention to the effects on the environment.

114267.explanation: School buildings are important meeting places in our villages and serve purposes beyond education. A good school requires a good building. Therefore, buildings and their interiors must be continuously improved. With this, we invest in the future.

114268.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

114272.explanation: Enforcement in Gemert-Bakel must be improved where necessary to maintain safety. But also to ensure everyone complies with other rules, for example, rules concerning a healthy living environment.

114273.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

114274.explanation: Domestic violence is an underestimated problem. We must collectively better care for (mainly) women and children who are victims. A women’s shelter can be part of that.

114275.explanation: What we mainly need: More homes that young people can afford. That should be our primary focus. We do not help young people by letting them borrow more.

114276.explanation: People who do not yet live here may have important, urgent reasons to want to live here. Think of parents who want to live closer to their child. They have the right to the same opportunities as people who already live here.

114278.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage. We assess per location whether holiday homes qualify for permanent residence.

114280.explanation: People are not on welfare because they do not want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra requirements and conditions only create more uncertainty.

114282.explanation: Gemert Bakel has the strictest special social assistance rules in the Netherlands. This is because Gemert defines poverty as 110% of the social minimum. Also, the way the ability to pay is calculated contributes to this. We want to change that.

114284.explanation: In the future, the share of electric cars will grow. It makes sense to have more charging points for that.

114285.explanation: The figures clearly show: a lower maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with reduced mobility, this makes a huge difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

114286.explanation: Unfortunately, we have a huge shortage of personnel in healthcare. It is not always possible to meet this understandable need of people.

114289.explanation: The dog tax is currently unfairly distributed. Dog owners in rural areas do not pay tax. Therefore, we want to abolish the dog tax like surrounding municipalities.

114290.explanation: We levy tourist tax to finance services for tourists. The tax is currently much lower than the national average. Neighboring municipalities also have higher taxes. Therefore, the tax can be raised slightly.

114296.explanation: De Eendracht has an important cultural function in our municipality. It must be able to continue to fulfill that. If adjustments are necessary, they must be made as sustainably as possible.

114300.explanation: Where appropriate, we build a little higher in the center of our historic village cores, but not higher than a maximum of 4 floors including a gabled roof. The appearance of the historic village cores must be preserved.

114304.explanation: It is the municipality’s task to enforce odor guidelines. There is no money to buy out farmers.

114248.explanation: The right person in the right place.

114249.explanation: As representatives of the people, we advocate the interests of the residents.

114252.explanation: Stores are already allowed in the municipality. Not every farm may have a campsite. In principle, an additional facility to generate extra income is allowed if it fits within the zoning plan.

114253.explanation: An energy collective may generate its own energy but will have to pay the start-up costs itself. The municipality can possibly provide administrative support with applications and such.

114255.explanation: Do not place limiting influx as the municipality’s responsibility.

114256.explanation: They must arrange housing themselves, preferably on their own site. But it can also be in the surrounding area.

114264.explanation: It depends on the location and number of houses. Spray circles are a possible problem here.

114267.explanation: We are already building several new schools and have a legal obligation to facilitate a healthy school environment.

114268.explanation: The Leergeld Foundation already takes care of this.

114272.explanation: Safety and enforcement must be much better in the municipality.

114273.explanation: Only deploy for nuisance and criminal activities, and it must be movable.

114274.explanation: As a municipality, we are too small for that.

114275.explanation: The municipality must commit itself to affordable home ownership.

114276.explanation: Our own residents have been waiting for a long time.

114278.explanation: Provided that the house and environment are suitable for it.

114280.explanation: Within people’s possibilities, it is good for social participation.

114284.explanation: But continued attention to parking for regular cars is needed.

114286.explanation: Nowadays, you should be happy if there is any care at all.

114289.explanation: We disagree that residents in rural areas do not pay dog tax. But for residents in the core, this has been studied and the conclusion was that there is no budget to abolish it.

114290.explanation: It must remain attractive for people to stay overnight here.

114296.explanation: We need to investigate the possibilities for a cultural center in the municipality. However, funding will have to be found for this.

114300.explanation: It depends on the location and what is considered high.

114248.explanation: D66 stands for equality and inclusion. A balanced composition of the executive board fits with this, but quality, local ties, and nomination by the municipal council are decisive.

114249.explanation: D66 believes in transparent decision-making and early influence. Residents must be involved early in plans so choices are made together and not afterwards via a referendum.

114252.explanation: Developments in rural areas must fit the landscape and livability. Many farmers want to become more sustainable and diversify. Secondary activities like a shop or campsite can contribute to a future-proof income model and reduce pressure on intensive agriculture.

114253.explanation: Energy is a spearhead for D66. Due to grid congestion and peak loads, local cooperation is needed. With financial support for citizen initiatives, we stimulate local networks in which residents, entrepreneurs, and farmers generate and use energy together.

114255.explanation: Gemert-Bakel must take its responsibility for humane reception. Good, small-scale, and humane shelter is part of an inclusive and reliable municipality.

114256.explanation: D66 believes employers are responsible for good housing, but not necessarily on their own company site. Housing must be decent and fit within the livability of villages.

114264.explanation: D66 wants a clear housing vision that designates suitable locations for residential construction. Fragmented housing in rural areas does not fit, although agricultural land can be part of that consideration.

114267.explanation: Good school buildings are essential. D66 invests in renovation, sustainability, and preservation of schools in all villages.

114268.explanation: Culture and talent development are part of equal opportunities. With subsidies, we ensure music lessons are accessible to children from low-income families.

114272.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and livability in villages and neighborhoods.

114273.explanation: D66 chooses prevention and social safety. Extra cameras often do not solve the causes of insecurity.

114274.explanation: A small-scale women's shelter is not realistic or effective in Gemert-Bakel. However, protection of vulnerable residents must always be well arranged through regional cooperation.

114275.explanation: Yes, with conditions. Starters need extra support to buy their first home, provided it is targeted and fair.

114276.explanation: Ties with the core village and livability are central in the allocation of social rental housing.

114278.explanation: Permanent residence undermines housing policy and livability. Recreation parks are not residential neighborhoods and lack the facilities, management, and quality that belong to them.

114280.explanation: D66 wants to stimulate and support social involvement, but not make it mandatory. Trust and guidance work better than coercion.

114282.explanation: By raising the income limit, we reach more people who need help and prevent problems from worsening.

114284.explanation: Through current policy, we are expanding charging facilities in residential areas to make electric driving accessible.

114285.explanation: In residential areas, we implement 30 km/h where possible. Not standard everywhere, but per street and village we assess what really contributes to safety.

114286.explanation: Freedom of choice is important where it is necessary and appropriate. Care should above all be effective, accessible, and well organized.

114289.explanation: The dog tax is outdated and adds little value.

114290.explanation: Tourism is valuable but must remain balanced. The tax rate requires careful consideration.

114296.explanation: Renewal of the activity center De Eendracht strengthens culture, social interaction, and village life.

114300.explanation: Medium-height building is possible in Gemert and Bakel. In smaller villages this requires careful and appropriate consideration.

114304.explanation: Buyouts can be a solution in case of serious nuisance, but not at any cost and not repeatedly without structural choices.

114248.explanation: Equal opportunities and broad representation are important to us. But a fixed 50/50 requirement is not always feasible in coalition formation. Choose based on quality and support, while actively promoting diversity in candidates and selection.

114249.explanation: For major decisions, residents must be able to directly voice their opinion. A referendum can help, provided there are clear rules: good information in advance, a clear question, sufficient turnout/support, and always follow-up and feedback from the council.

114252.explanation: We welcome new initiatives in the countryside, but 'always allowing' is not possible. It must fit the environment and rules: traffic/parking, nature, safety, and nuisance. So room for customization and quick permits where possible, but no automatic approval.

114253.explanation: We support local energy initiatives, but support must be targeted. The municipality assists with permits, grid connection, and can provide a start-up subsidy/loan if the plan is feasible and the yield benefits the local area. No blank check.

114255.explanation: A reception center (AZC) in Gemert-Bakel is only considered if there is a fair distribution and all municipalities participate. First clarity on regional agreements, capacity, and conditions (safety, support, location). No voluntary lead role.

114256.explanation: Employers are responsible for decent housing, but not always on their own premises. Choose locations that suit the environment and facilities, with strict quality requirements, management, and enforcement. This prevents nuisance and uncontrolled growth, keeping things properly regulated.

114264.explanation: Yes, with clear rules. Only where it fits housing needs and landscape, with good accessibility and without extra odor/nuisance. Link to quality improvements: demolition/yard beautification and strict assessment.

114267.explanation: Good school buildings are the basis for learning and health. Invest systematically in maintenance and sustainability, prioritizing where safety and indoor climate are tight. Use one multi-year plan and seize opportunities for co-financing.

114268.explanation: Music lessons give children opportunities and strengthen culture and associations. With subsidies, every primary school child should be able to participate, hassle-free. Organize this via schools and local providers, with a simple scheme so it is really used.

114272.explanation: More community enforcement officers (boa’s) on the street make the municipality safer and ensure reports are genuinely followed up. Focus on nuisance, youth, waste, and minor crime, with visibility in all neighborhoods. Clear priorities and results noticeable to residents.

114273.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance at hotspots can help with nuisance and investigation. Only where necessary, with clear objectives, privacy rules, short retention periods, and fixed evaluation. This complements boa’s, not replaces them.

114274.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must be able to leave safely immediately. Realize a (regional) women’s shelter with 24/7 housing close to home, with guidance and quick coordination with Safe at Home, police, and housing corporations.

114275.explanation: Starters are currently too often left out. A starter’s loan helps first-time buyers bridge the financing gap. Focus this on affordable homes and genuine starters, with clear conditions and a simple application process. See election program.

114276.explanation: Residents of Gemert-Bakel should more often get priority for social housing, within legal rules. This helps starters and movers within their own neighborhoods. Do this transparently, with clear criteria and space for urgent cases.

114278.explanation: Yes, where it can be done safely and responsibly. Allow permanent residence after assessment of fire safety, housing quality, facilities, and livability, with agreements with the parking owner and enforcement against abuses. See election program.

114280.explanation: It depends on the situation. If there is labor potential, an appropriate offer belongs to it and people can be held accountable for it. But those who cannot due to health, caregiving, or problems receive support. Goal: participation, not punishment.

114282.explanation: Too many families currently just fall outside the net. Raising the income limit also helps low-income workers and prevents debts. We also want to raise the special social assistance threshold to 130% instead of the current 110%.

114284.explanation: More charging points are needed, especially for residents without driveways. Allocate extra spots based on demand and parking pressure, smartly spread over all neighborhoods. Ensure clear rules (tackle parking at charging spots) and link this to a clear charging policy.

114285.explanation: 30 km/h as the standard speed in residential streets makes it safer and calmer. On some main access roads 50 km/h can remain if the road is designed accordingly and demonstrably safe. So: 30 as standard, with clear exceptions.

114286.explanation: We take faith, culture, and beliefs into account where possible, but 'always' is not possible due to scarcity and supply. Quality and accessibility come first. We do promote diverse offerings and good matching, without exclusion.

114289.explanation: As long as dog waste and nuisance remain a problem, abolishing the tax is illogical: polluter pays. First enforce rules and ensure sufficient facilities. Only reconsider the tax if things really improve.

114290.explanation: The tourism tax in Gemert-Bakel is relatively low. Therefore, it may be increased. Condition: the proceeds go to a stimulation fund for livability and better facilities in the neighborhoods, so residents and visitors visibly benefit.

114296.explanation: We have already invested €1 million to get De Eendracht ahead for 10 years. Financial priorities are now elsewhere. First utilize the effect of that investment and prioritize systematically within the overall accommodation policy. Don’t promise what you can’t deliver.

114300.explanation: High residential towers are allowed if they are located in the right places. Close to facilities and public transport, with good integration, sufficient parking, greenery, and safe routes. This way you can add homes faster without unnecessarily burdening the village.

114304.explanation: Odor problems must be addressed, but buyouts are not a standard tool. First: enforce permits and standards and manage measures. Buyouts can only be a last resort for demonstrable structural nuisance, with independent assessment and clear cost limits.

statements.title: Gemert-Bakel must buy out livestock farmers who cause odor nuisance.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Gemert-Bakel must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Gemert-Bakel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety must be deployed in Gemert-Bakel.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: More camera surveillance must be introduced in Gemert-Bakel.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) must be established in Gemert-Bakel.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in Gemert-Bakel.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Gemert-Bakel must renovate activity center De Eendracht.
